Shared Decision-Making During Surgical Consultations for Indeterminate Thyroid Nodules

evidence_entities.text: Thyroid nodules are common, present in over 60% of the population. Despite thyroid carcinoma being the seventh most common cancer globally, a large proportion of thyroid nodules are found incidentally in asymptomatic patients and represent benign disease.

nodules showing atypia of undetermined significance (AUS, Bethesda-III) or follicular neoplasm (FN, Bethesda-IV) are considered indeterminate in their risk for malignancy, with rates of 13% to 30% and 23% to 34%, respectively

management guidelines released by the American Thyroid Association have allowed for repeat fine needle aspiration (FNA) biopsy, diagnostic hemithyroidectomy, ultrasonographic surveillance, or molecular diagnostic testing
Patient-perceived SDM was high [mean = 94.38, standard deviation (SD) = 7.61], while physician-perceived SDM was lower in comparison (mean = 83.21, SD = 9.14)
Clinically significant DC (DCS score ≥25) was reported by 13.2% of patients
There was a medium negative correlation between patient-perceived SDM involvement and DC (Pearson r = −0.311, P = 0.008)
